provisions.title: Design of public seal
provisions.content: There shall be a public seal which shall show the armorial ensigns of Uganda circumscribed with the words "Republic of Uganda".

provisions.title: Control of public seal
provisions.content: The President shall be the custodian of the public seal and may by statutory instrument under his or her hand— (a) assign the custody of the public seal to a Minister who shall countersign any document to which the public seal is affixed; and (b) give other directions as to the manner in which it shall be kept and used.

provisions.title: Validity of existing documents
provisions.content: Nothing in this Act shall affect the validity of any instrument lawfully sealed before the commencement of this Act. History of this document 31 December 2000 this version Chapter 256 Revised Laws 2000 Consolidation 01 October 1963 Commenced Related documents [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Facebook Page Visit our Twitter/X profile About ULII ULII is a project of the Law Reporting Committee of the Judiciary of Uganda. ULII aims to bridge the existing gap in public access to the law of Uganda. Working with the AfricanLII and in collaboration with other partners, such as the Free Access to Law Movement, ULII's major aim is to be a user-friendly portal of all publicly available legal information in Uganda. The Judiciary of Uganda considers access to its decisions and the law upon which they are based as key tenets of its key mission: Justice for All . ULII is also an end user interface for the Electronic Court Case Management Information System.
